metadata.description: A CPU component. First-class entity form of the §26 component model — a parent carries the cpu rollup and MAY also contain Hardware.Processor entities. device_class distinguishes a physical socket from a virtual CPU presented to a guest. outputs-exempt: opaque inventory component — its attributes are Discovered-state facts on the containing host; it publishes no realize-time binding surface (rule 36(b) documented exclusion).

context.purpose: Inventories a CPU — a physical socket or a vCPU presented to a guest — as a first-class component when the host rollup is not enough.
context.plain_description: One processor as its own record: model, cores, threads, architecture, clock. Most estates only need the aggregate CPU numbers already carried on the host; this type exists for when a socket must be individually addressable (asset tracking, heterogeneous sockets). device_class distinguishes the physical socket from a virtual CPU presented to a guest. Nothing binds to a processor at runtime, so it publishes no outputs — it is inventory.
use_when: You need per-socket inventory (exact model, position, serial) beyond the host's aggregate core/thread rollup.
You need virtual CPUs presented to a guest tracked as components.
not_for: Ordinary capacity accounting — the cpu rollup on Compute.BareMetalHost covers that without per-socket records.
works_with: Compute.BareMetalHost — the host the socket is installed in, which carries the reconciled rollup.
Compute.VirtualMachine — the guest a virtual CPU is presented to.

device_class.description: Device realization (common-elements.md §7). physical = a socket/package; virtual = vCPU.

partition_mechanism.description: Only when device_class=partition: e.g. cpu-pinning / cpu-set carved from a parent.

location.description: Socket designation within the parent, e.g. CPU1 (Redfish Socket). Primary discriminator.

model.description: Processor model, e.g. AMD EPYC 7443P (Redfish Model).

relationships.name: host
relationships.description: The host this processor is installed in; parent carries the reconciled cpu rollup (§26).

relationships.name: guest
relationships.description: For device_class=virtual, the guest the vCPU is presented to.

adopts.role: processor component vocabulary (cores, threads, architecture, speed, socket)
